name: 100_scoped_ab
description: A/B: SCOPED-assembled context vs flat assembly. Every judge assertion runs paired (scoped=false control, scoped=true treatment). Gap-analysis questions are the class where structure should win.

steps.user_message: I'm Adam, founder of IIMAGINE - 7 people, desktop AI companion for small business owners. MRR is $85k, runway 14 months.
My main objective this year: reach 500 paying customers by September 30. It's a priority 8 out of 10 for me. We're at 300 paying customers right now.
Biggest obstacles: engineering is at full capacity this quarter, and nobody on the team has outbound sales experience.
On the plus side we have a large untapped email list from the beta waitlist, and Sarah Chen our CTO is exceptional at product.
Customer update: we crossed 340 paying customers this week.
I also still hold my zero-debt rule - we grow from revenue only, non-negotiable.

challenges.text: engineering at full capacity this quarter
no outbound sales experience on the team

enablers.text: large untapped email list from beta waitlist

steps.label: CONTROL gap-analysis: hit the target?
steps.question: Can I still realistically hit my paying-customer target for the year?
required_facts: Engineering is at full capacity / sales experience is lacking

steps.label: SCOPED gap-analysis: hit the target?
steps.question: Can I still realistically hit my paying-customer target for the year?
required_facts: Engineering is at full capacity / sales experience is lacking
The remaining gap to the target (about 160 customers) or the required pace is stated explicitly
How much time remains before the deadline is stated explicitly

steps.label: CONTROL tradeoff: side project vs goal
steps.question: A partner wants us to co-build a small integration this quarter - it would take meaningful engineering time. Should I say yes?
required_facts: Engineering is at full capacity this quarter
There is a high-priority customer-growth objective with a deadline

steps.label: SCOPED tradeoff: side project vs goal
steps.question: A partner wants us to co-build a small integration this quarter - it would take meaningful engineering time. Should I say yes?
required_facts: Engineering is at full capacity this quarter
The customer-growth objective, its priority level, and its deadline are all present

steps.label: CONTROL focus question
steps.question: What should I focus on this month?

steps.label: SCOPED focus question
steps.question: What should I focus on this month?
required_facts: The constraints (capacity, sales gap) and enablers (email list) relevant to closing the gap

steps.label: SCOPED no-regression: unrelated advisory question
steps.question: The bank offered a $200k line of credit at good rates. Should I take it?
required_facts: The owner has a firm zero-debt preference

steps.label: false-match guard: off-goal query gets zero briefs
steps.query: what should we order for the team lunch on Friday

steps.user_message: Update - we just crossed 380 paying customers.
steps.assistant_response: 380 now. The pace is picking up.

steps.label: superseded-fact probe: brief never asserts 340 as current after 380 lands
steps.query: can I still hit my customer target
steps.note: Phase 1 validates the DROP path (provenance refresh); the REPLACE path is Phase 2's update loop.
